question_text: A cyclist travels 8 km in the first 20 minutes of a journey, then 12 km in the next 40 minutes. Calculate the average speed for the entire journey in km/h.
explanation: Total distance = 8 + 12 = 20 km. Total time = 20 + 40 = 60 minutes = 1 hour. Average speed = 20 km ÷ 1 h = 12 km/h. Remember to add all distances and times together before calculating average speed.
